title: Fibrous material baling system
abstract: Various embodiments of the present disclosure provide a fibrous material baling system that includes a pivotable bale-transporter guide (100) that moves with the bale transporter during bale extraction and that includes a controller configured to control movement of the bale transporter (200) using adaptive speed control functionality responsive to the statuses of the bale press (10) and the bagger (1000) to prevent unnecessary movement of the bale transporter.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A baling system comprising: a bale-transporter guide pivotably mountable to a substrate; and a bale transporter mounted to the bale-transporter guide such that the bale transporter can move along and relative to the bale-transporter guide. 2. The baling system of claim 1, wherein the bale-transporter guide comprises a guide assembly comprising a base mountable to the substrate and a rail comprising a first end pivotably connected to the base and a second end. 3. The baling system of claim 2, wherein the bale transporter is slidably mounted to the rail. 4. The baling system of claim 3, wherein the bale-transporter guide further comprises a collapsible and expandable bellows extending between the second end of the rail and a first portion of the bale transporter, wherein the bellows is positioned and configured to cover part of the rail extending between the second end of the rail and the first portion of the bale transporter as the bale transporter moves along and relative to the bale-transporter guide. 5. The baling system of claim 4, wherein the bale-transporter guide further comprises a collapsible and expandable second bellows extending between the first end of the rail and a second portion of the bale transporter, wherein the second bellows is positioned and configured to cover part of the rail extending between the first end of the rail and the second 

 portion of the bale transporter as the bale transporter moves along and relative to the bale- transporter guide. 6. The baling system of claim 3, wherein the rail comprises a linear gear comprising multiple teeth. 7. The baling system of claim 6, wherein the bale transporter comprises a bale- guide-assembly attacher slidably mounted to the rail, the bale -guide-assembly attacher comprising a spur gear comprising multiple teeth that mesh with the teeth of the linear gear of the rail. 8. The baling system of claim 7, wherein the bale transporter comprises an actuator operably connected to the spur gear of the bale -guide-assembly attacher and configured to rotate the spur gear of the bale -guide-assembly attacher to cause the bale transporter to move along and relative to the bale-transporter guide. 9. The baling system of claim 8, further comprising a second guide assembly comprising a second base mountable to the substrate and a second rail comprising a first end pivotably connected to the base and a second end, wherein the bale transporter is slidably mounted to the second rail, wherein the second rail comprises a second linear gear comprising multiple teeth, wherein the bale transporter further comprises a second bale -guide-assembly attacher slidably mounted to the second rail, the second bale -guide-assembly attacher comprising a second spur gear comprising multiple teeth that mesh with the teeth of the second linear gear of the second rail, wherein the actuator is operably connected to the second spur gear of the 

 second bale -guide-assembly attacher and configured to rotate the second spur gear of the second bale -guide-assembly attacher to cause the bale transporter to move along and relative to the bale-transporter guide. 10. The baling system of claim 1, wherein the bale-transporter guide comprises a rack and the bale transporter comprises a pinion, wherein the bale transporter is mounted to the bale- transporter guide such that the pinion engages the rack and such that rotation of the pinion causes the bale transporter to move along and relative to the bale-transporter guide. 11. The baling system of claim 1, further comprising a bale-transporter controller operably connected to the bale transporter to control movement of the bale transporter along and relative to the bale-transporter guide. 12. The baling system of claim 11, wherein the bale-transporter controller is configured to control movement of the bale transporter responsive to information received from a bale press controller of a bale press. 13. The baling system of claim 12, wherein the information comprises a status of the bale press. 14. A method of operating a baling system, the method comprising: receiving, from a bale press controller, information regarding a status of the bale press; using the information regarding the status of the bale press to control movement of a bale transporter such that the bale transporter arrives at the bale press within a designated period of time before or after a bale is ready for extraction from the bale press; and 

 extracting the bale from the bale press. 15. The method of claim 14, further comprising: receiving, from a bagger controller, information regarding a status of a bagger; using the information regarding the status of the bagger to control movement of a bale transporter such that the bale transporter arrives at the bagger within a second designated period of time before or after the bagger is ready to receive the bale; and discharging the bale from the bale transporter into the bagger.
